Our Story

A street with 200 years of history

Chelsea holds one of Manhattan's best-kept secrets — entire blocks of 19th-century brownstones, nearly unchanged since they were first built.

The story begins with Clement Clarke Moore — the poet who wrote "'Twas the Night Before Christmas" and, more importantly, shaped Chelsea with his own hands. In the 1820s, he subdivided his family's orchards into residential lots, establishing strict covenants: no factories, no commerce — only elegant homes for New York families.

The West 22nd Street where our garden sits is part of that legacy. The street's brownstones date back to the mid-19th century, and their impressive row of warm sandstone facades still evoke Victorian New York — one of the most cinematic blocks in all of Manhattan, frequently used as a film and TV backdrop.

"Chelsea is proof that New York knows how to keep beauty in its most unexpected corners."

The neighborhood went through decades of transformation: industrial enclave, home to underground artists, epicenter of contemporary art galleries. Today Chelsea is simultaneously cosmopolitan and intimately residential — with the High Line elevated above its streets, Chelsea Market born from a cookie factory, and 19th-century townhouses standing alongside glass towers.

West 22nd Street, between 9th and 10th Avenue, has received special recognition in the New York press as one of the most beautiful and best-preserved brownstone blocks in Manhattan. The cherry blossoms in spring and golden foliage in autumn make it one of the most photographed streets in the city.

The Studio

Your home in Manhattan

A private studio apartment with an independent entrance, full kitchen, and garden access — an experience unlike any hotel.

Situated at garden level in a historic brownstone, the studio offers complete privacy — you have your own entrance, and never need to pass through the main building's corridors. It is, quite literally, your home.

The fully equipped kitchen means you can cook your own breakfast, store groceries from the Chelsea Market right around the corner, and live like a New Yorker — not a hotel tourist.

The outdoor space — the garden that gives this place its name — is a green sanctuary where you can have coffee, read, or simply breathe. In a city with New York's pace, having a private garden is a rare luxury.

✓ Separate, private entrance
✓ Full private kitchen
✓ Private bathroom
✓ Exclusive outdoor garden
✓ Wi-Fi gratuito + TV a cabo
✓ King or Twin beds
✓ Scrupulously cleaned between stays
✓ Quiet residential street

Neighborhood

Chelsea and its
treasures within reach

Everything that makes New York unforgettable is within walking distance of our door.

Highlights
Art & Culture
Parks & Recreation
Shopping & Markets
🚂
~8 min walk

Penn Station / Moynihan Hall

The largest rail hub in the US, connecting Manhattan to the world — Amtrak, NJ Transit and LIRR. For the 2026 World Cup, it's the arrival point for most visitors. Walking distance from our door.

🏟️
~10 min walk

Madison Square Garden

The world's most famous arena is an easy walk away. Concerts, NBA, NHL and historic events happen here — including FIFA World Cup 2026 matches.

🌿
~4 min walk

The High Line

The world's most famous elevated park, built on the tracks of an abandoned 1930s freight railway. Stretching 1.5 km, it offers unique city views, art installations and native gardens. It draws 8 million visitors per year.

🍪
~6 min walk

Chelsea Market

Built in the former Nabisco factory — where the Oreo cookie was invented in 1912. Today it's a gourmet food hall with 6 million visitors per year, with restaurants, markets and shops in a preserved industrial setting. Google bought the building for $2.4 billion in 2018.

⛵
~10 min walk

Hudson River Park

5 km of parkland along the Hudson — cycling, kayaking, outdoor concerts and the best sunset views in Manhattan. Includes Little Island, an artificial island opened in 2021 with an amphitheater design over the water.

🎨
~5 min walk

Chelsea Galleries

Chelsea is the world capital of contemporary art, with over 200 galleries clustered in the western part of the neighborhood. Artists like Jeff Koons, Damien Hirst and Cindy Sherman have (or have had) galleries here. Thursday evening openings are a neighborhood tradition.

💃
~8 min walk

Joyce Theater

One of the most important dance theaters in the US, housed in a 1941 Art Déco movie house. It presents independent dance companies from around the world, with programming that blends tradition and avant-garde.

🍃
~15 min walk

General Theological Seminary

Founded in 1827 by Clement Clarke Moore — the poet who shaped Chelsea. Its Gothic campus with interior gardens is one of Manhattan's best-kept secrets. Open to the public at specific hours.

📚
~10 min walk

192 Books

One of New York's most beloved independent bookstores, located right in the middle of Chelsea's galleries. Frequent author readings and launches.

🏗️
~8 min walk

Frank Gehry IAC Building

The legendary architect Frank Gehry's first building in New York — a flowing white glass sculpture on 10th Avenue. A landmark of contemporary design that's worth the walk.

🎭
~12 min walk

Flatiron District & Greenwich Village

Two of New York's most iconic neighborhoods just minutes away. The 1902 Flatiron Building and the cobblestone streets of the Village are must-sees for any visit.

🎪
~12 min walk

Javits Convention Center

One of the largest convention centers in the US, home to major international events. Recently expanded with a green roof that serves as habitat for bees and migratory birds — a unique ecological project in Manhattan.

🌊
~10 min walk

Chelsea Piers

Built in 1910, these historic piers were the arrival terminal for great ocean liners — this is where the Titanic was supposed to dock. Today it's a sports complex with an ice rink, golf range, gym and Hudson-view restaurants.

🌺
~15 min walk

Little Island

New York's newest artificial island, opened in 2021. 2.4 acres of gardens, an amphitheater and sculptures float over the Hudson on structures that look like giant tulips. Free and spectacular.

🥗
~15 min walk

Union Square Farmers Market

One of America's largest farmers' markets, running since 1976. On Wednesdays, Fridays and Saturdays (and Sundays), farmers from the Northeast bring fresh produce, flowers and crafts. Saturday is the most vibrant day.

🌳
~15 min walk

Madison Square Park

The historic park where the Flatiron Building meets the original Madison Square Garden. It features rotating outdoor sculptures and is where the first Shake Shack opened in 2004, with a line that wrapped around the park.

🍎
~5 min walk

Apple Store & Whole Foods

For everyday shopping, the Whole Foods on 10th Avenue is the nearest premium supermarket. The Chelsea Apple Store is the go-to for any tech support needs.

👗
~15 min walk

Meatpacking District

The former meatpacking district has become the address of New York's coolest boutiques. Alexander McQueen, Diane von Furstenberg, Stella McCartney — all here. The bar and restaurant scene is one of the best in the city.

🍫
~6 min walk

Chelsea Market Shops

Beyond the restaurants, Chelsea Market houses unique shops: flower boutiques, an award-winning wine shop, artisan chocolatier, independent bookstore and much more — all in the same historic cookie factory setting.

🌐
~8 min walk

Chelsea Galleries & Studios

Many Chelsea galleries have adjacent art shops — an opportunity to take home work by international artists at more accessible prices than auctions. Wandering along West 24th–26th Street is free and fascinating.

Dining

The finest restaurants in the neighborhood

Chelsea has one of Manhattan's most diverse food scenes — from the morning bagel to a late dinner with natural wine.

All
Italian
American
Bakery & Café
Other
Cookshop
American · Farm-to-table
10th Ave at 20th St
One of Manhattan's best farm-to-table restaurants. The weekend brunch is lively and always packed — there's usually a line, but it's worth the wait.
Empire Diner
American · Ícone
10th Ave at 22nd St
A legendary Art Déco diner — it appeared in films like Taxi Driver and episodes of Law & Order. Classic New York, any time of day.
Bottino
Italian · Galeria crowd
10th Ave btwn 24th/25th
A favorite of the art dealers and curators from the surrounding galleries. Charming back garden in summer, simple and well-executed Italian cooking.
Don Giovanni
Pizza / Italian
9th Ave btwn 22nd/23rd
Authentic Neapolitan pizza in the heart of Chelsea. Relaxed atmosphere, reasonable prices and generous portions — a neighborhood favorite.
La Bergamote
French Bakery
9th Ave at 20th St
An authentic French pâtisserie in Manhattan — butter croissants, éclairs and quiches that transport you straight to Paris. Breakfast here is a must.
Tia Pol
Spanish Tapas
10th Ave btwn 22nd/23rd
Spanish tapas that would rival any bar in San Sebastián. Natural wine and an intimate atmosphere — always packed and lively. Reservations recommended.
The Mermaid Inn
Seafood
277 10th Ave at 24th St
The best lobster roll in Chelsea. New England fish shack atmosphere with a refined Manhattan touch. Fresh oysters daily.
Shukette
Mediterranean
290 9th Ave at 24th St
Vibrant Mediterranean cooking with seasonal vegetables as the star. One of the New York Times' favorites in Chelsea — hard to book but a memorable experience.
Socarrat
Paella · Wine Bar
259 W 19th St
The best paella in New York, full stop. Valenciana, black ink, mushroom — the crispy bottom crust (socarrat) is the treasure of the dish. Impressive Spanish wine list.
Billy's Bakery
Cakes & Cupcakes
9th Ave btwn 21st/22nd
A slice of carrot cake or a red velvet cupcake here are rituals for Chelsea residents. Retro American style, unforgettable flavors.
Momoya
Japanese · Sushi
7th Ave at 21st St
One of the most consistent sushi spots in Manhattan outside Midtown. Calm atmosphere, fresh fish and omakase at reasonable prices by New York standards.
Sullivan St. Bakery
Artisan Bakery
10th Ave at 24th St
Jim Lahey revolutionized American artisan bread — his no-knead technique influenced bakers worldwide. The focaccia and pane bianco are masterpieces.
Zucker's Bagels
Bagels / Deli
8th Ave btwn 22nd/23rd
A bagel with cream cheese and smoked salmon is mandatory any morning in New York. Zucker's uses a traditional recipe and artisan baking — bagels as they should be.
Pastai
Italian · Massas
9th Ave btwn 21st/22nd
Handmade fresh pasta in an informal osteria — the kind of place locals keep secret and visitors love when they discover it.
City Winery
Vinho & Dining
Chelsea Piers
Winery, restaurant and live music venue all in one. They produce their own wines in barrels you can see from the dining room. Acoustic shows by well-known artists almost every night.
Getting Around

Connected to
all of New York

🚇

Subway & Bus (MTA)

Lines A, C, E on 8th Avenue (1 minute) and lines 1, 2, 3 on 7th Avenue (5 min). Connects to all of Manhattan and every borough, 24 hours a day.

🚂

Penn Station / Moynihan Hall

Walking distance (~8 min). Amtrak (national), NJ Transit and LIRR. For the 2026 World Cup: direct arrival from JFK, Newark and Boston with no train changes.

🚌

Port Authority Bus Terminal

~10 min walk. Terminal de ônibus interestaduais — conexões para toda a costa leste dos EUA, incluindo aeroportos.

🚕

Taxi & Rideshare

Uber, Lyft and yellow cabs available 24/7. The quiet residential street allows for immediate pickup and dropoff at the door.

🚲

Citi Bike

Shared bicycle stations on the street. Explore the High Line, Hudson River Park and the Village by bike — the most enjoyable way to see Chelsea.

✈️

Aeroportos

JFK: AirTrain + subway ~50 min. Newark: NJ Transit from Penn Station ~20 min. LaGuardia: M60 bus ~45 min or taxi.
